# por/dual parity audit (0.61.0-1)

Read-only snapshot audit of every place the `dual` workspace
topology (code repo + `.claude/` companion, cross-linked by
`ochid:` trailers, configured by `.vc-config.toml`) gets a
privileged code path over `por` (Plain Old Repo — single code
repo, no `.claude/`, no `.vc-config.toml`). Snapshot taken at
commit `463e6bd9` (`docs: prep por/dual parity audit
(0.61.0-0)`). Parent design item is T5 in `todo.md` [[1]];
design stub is `por-dual-parity.md` [[2]].

- Scope: every subcommand, the `--por` flag's reach, the
  ochid-touching helpers, test fixtures.
- Out of scope: proposing equalization shape (each finding
  carries a one-sentence sketch only — concrete fixes are
  follow-up cycles).
- Method: grep + read-through. No code changes.

## Status

**Pre-implementation design snapshot.** This document
captures the audit findings (`## 1`–`## 8`), the
commonality analysis (`## Commonality`), and the
negotiated design (`## Feature axes`) at the end of cycle
0.61.0. The matrix, the per-axis Decisions blocks, and
the resolution chain are the *current target*, not the
final shape.

**The implementation will diverge from this.** Every
design stub does — implementation surfaces edge cases,
forces compromises, and reveals constraints the design
pass didn't anticipate. Treat what's here as the starting
position for 0.62.0+ equalization cycles, not a contract
the code must conform to. When implementation finds the
design wrong, update the design (or annotate the
divergence in chores) — don't bend code to fit a stale
sketch.

## 1. `options_flags/por.rs` — the `--por` gate

- Files touched:
  - `src/options_flags/por.rs` (whole file, 1-20).
  - Consumers: `src/init.rs:13,61-65,592-596,1199-1203`,
    `src/clone.rs:26,48-51,70,81,143-163`,
    `src/init/params.rs:39,60`.
  - Test references only:
    `src/test_helpers.rs:24,105,196`,
    `src/init/tests.rs` (many).
- Divergence: `PorFlag` is consumed by **exactly two**
  subcommands — `init` and `clone`. No other subcommand
  surfaces `--por` (`grep "PorFlag" src/` shows zero other
  hits). Doc on the flag itself is creation-time framed: "at
  creation time" — there is no equivalent runtime flag for
  the post-creation commands (`push`, `sync`, `finalize`,
  `validate-desc`, `fix-desc`, `show`, etc.) to declare
  "treat this workspace as por".
- Severity / category: *Defaulting* + *Feature gap*. Absent
  flag = dual; `--por` is a creation-only override; runtime
  commands infer topology indirectly (presence /
  `workspace.other-repo` value of `.vc-config.toml`).
- Equalization sketch: The bot thinks the path is to either
  promote `PorFlag` into a shared option flag plumbed
  through every command, or commit to topology-from-config
  uniformly (no `--por` runtime flag, all commands read
  `default_scope` / `.vc-config.toml` and branch from
  there). The latter is closer to today's `sync`-style
  resolution.

## 2. `init` — dual privileged at the orchestration layer

- Files touched:
  - `src/init.rs` — `plan_init` (583-641), `create_por`
    (1217-1265), `create_dual` (1280-1380),
    `write_por_vc_config` / `write_por_gitignore` (355-363),
    `write_code_config` / `write_session_config` (379-391),
    dispatch (1199-1203).
  - `src/init/params.rs:39,60` — `por: bool` field.
- Divergence: Symmetric on the surface (`create_por` /
  `create_dual` are sibling orchestrators), but the
  defaulting is asymmetric:
  - `plan_init` synthesizes `Scope(vec![Side::Code])` when
    `params.por` else `Scope(vec![Side::Code, Side::Bot])`
    (init.rs:592-596).
  - `--config` is rejected unless `--por` is set
    (init.rs:608-613, "`--config` is only valid with
    `--por` (dual configs are per-side and unconditional)")
    — dual gets implicit per-side configs; por gets
    user-controllable config and an unconditional
    `.gitignore`.
  - Dispatch defaults to `create_dual` (init.rs:1199-1203:
    `if params.por { create_por } else { create_dual }`).
  - The dry-run printout treats dual as the baseline and
    emits "(skipped — …)" lines for por
    (init.rs:1184-1196).
- Severity / category: *Defaulting* (medium — dual is the
  default branch; por is the opt-in branch with extra
  config-knob asymmetry).
- Equalization sketch: The bot thinks the two sibling
  orchestrators could share a common config-writing layer
  with topology-driven side selection — but the bigger ask
  is reframing the dispatch so neither shape is "the
  default" at the type level (e.g. a `Topology { Por,
  Dual }` enum threaded through, with the default decided by
  user config, per the deferred user-config-topology-default
  proposal).

## 3. `clone` — same shape as init

- Files touched:
  - `src/clone.rs` — `clone_repo` (113-167), `clone_one`
    (180-196), `clone_dual` (202-226), `CloneParams`
    (67-85).
- Divergence: Mirror of init at the dispatch layer
  (clone.rs:156-163: `if params.por { clone_one } else {
  clone_dual }`). `clone_one` clones a single repo and
  verifies tracking; `clone_dual` clones code + bot
  (deriving the bot URL via `derive_session_url`) and
  installs the symlink. The bot side is mandatory in
  `clone_dual` ("no graceful skip — both sides required by
  the default dual shape").
- Severity / category: *Defaulting* (medium). Dual is the
  default; `clone_one` is reachable only via `--por`.
- Equalization sketch: Same as init — topology choice
  could be lifted off the flag and onto the
  user/workspace-config layer; the two orchestrators are
  already cleanly factored, so the wiring change is small.

## 4. `push` — dual-only, no por support at all

- Files touched:
  - `src/push.rs` (whole file).
  - `src/push/integration_tests.rs` (whole file).
- Divergence: `push` is dual-baked from the ground up. No
  `--por` flag is exposed; `PushArgs` (push.rs:127-183) has
  no topology selector. The state machine declares stages
  `CommitClaude`, `BookmarkBoth`, `PushApp`,
  `FinalizeClaude` (push.rs:46-63, 100-113); the helper
  `claude_path(workspace_root)` (push.rs:666-668)
  hard-codes `<root>/.claude` as the session repo; commit
  bodies are stamped with `ochid: /.claude/<chid>` and
  `ochid: /<chid>` (push.rs:1106, 1169). Sanity checks
  (`verify_state_sanity`, `verify_completion_sanity`)
  validate both repos' bookmarks against
  `state.app_chid` / `state.claude_chid`
  (push.rs:1314-1501). Finalize is shelled out against
  `--repo .claude` (push.rs:1265-1290). The 1:1
  symmetric-WC-commits assumption noted in `todo.md > #1`
  lives here.
- Severity / category: *Feature gap* (high). Running
  `vc-x1 push` against a por workspace would try to
  resolve `.claude/...` paths that don't exist.
- Equalization sketch: The bot thinks a por-mode push
  collapses to "preflight → review → message →
  commit-app → bookmark-app → push-app" (drop the
  `*_claude` stages) — a topology check at entry, plus
  conditional stage skipping, would cover most of it; the
  ochid trailer omission is the only nontrivial wrinkle.

## 5. `sync` — partially por-aware

- Files touched:
  - `src/sync.rs:17,160-169` (`resolve_params_to_repos`).
  - `src/common.rs:569-653` (`find_workspace_root`,
    `default_scope`, `scope_to_repos`).
  - `src/sync/integration_tests.rs` (whole file).
- Divergence: Sync is the one place por is a first-class
  case at runtime. `default_scope` (common.rs:604-616)
  returns `Scope([Code])` when there is no workspace root
  (POR) or no `workspace.other-repo` key; returns
  `Scope([Code, Bot])` only when the dual marker is
  present. `scope_to_repos` (common.rs:626-653) resolves
  `Side::Code` to cwd's `.` when `workspace_root` is None,
  so a por run still operates on cwd. The `-R` flag (per
  done #5) lets a por or single-repo path be supplied
  directly (sync.rs:160-169). The privileging that
  remains: the `--scope=bot` path errors out for por
  (common.rs:638-647), as it should, but the *default*
  reads the workspace marker — a por workspace silently
  falls through to "current directory only".
- Severity / category: *Defaulting* (low). Behavior is
  correct for por; the asymmetry is just that dual gets a
  richer default while por gets a fallback default.
- Equalization sketch: Likely no change needed; the
  `default_scope` shape already encodes the topology
  fallback cleanly.

## 6. `finalize` — topology-neutral, dual-shaped use site

- Files touched:
  - `src/finalize.rs` (whole file; spot-checked 1-100,
    580-650).
- Divergence: `finalize` itself is a single-repo operator
  — it takes `--repo <path>` and operates on that one repo
  (squash + bookmark + push, optionally detached). No
  topology branching, no `.vc-config.toml` reads, no
  `.claude/` assumption inside the body. The *callers* are
  dual-shaped, however:
  - `push::stage_finalize_claude` (push.rs:1251-1290)
    invokes it with `--repo .claude`.
  - The user/bot workflow's "one push = one `.claude`
    commit" cadence is dual-only; por has nothing to
    finalize since there is no `.claude/`.
- Severity / category: *Coverage gap* (low). Code is
  topology-neutral; the surrounding workflow is dual-shaped
  by convention.
- Equalization sketch: No changes inside `finalize`. The
  bot thinks the por equivalent is "finalize is unused";
  documenting that explicitly (and/or making
  `push --por` skip stage-finalize-claude) would close the
  workflow gap.

## 7. ochid helpers — dual-assumed unconditionally

- Files touched:
  - `src/desc_helpers.rs:12-42` (`VC_CONFIG_FILE`,
    `other_repo_from_config`, `ochid_prefix_from_config`).
  - `src/validate_desc.rs:121-138` (load other repo, read
    ochid prefix, then validate every commit's trailer).
  - `src/fix_desc.rs:140-160,295-318` (same pattern; also
    `--fallback /.claude/lost`).
  - `src/chid.rs`, `src/desc.rs`, `src/list.rs`,
    `src/show.rs` — neutral; they don't read ochids, only
    accept `-R .claude` as a repo path. The flexibility is
    in shared `CommonArgs` (anchored at `common.rs:674-684,
    `resolve_repos`).
- Divergence: `validate_desc` and `fix_desc` *require* a
  `.vc-config.toml` with `workspace.other-repo`; running
  them in a por workspace immediately errors with
  "missing workspace.other-repo in .vc-config.toml"
  (desc_helpers.rs:18-24) or fails to open
  `<repo>/.vc-config.toml`. There is no opt-out, no
  topology check, no skip-when-por path. The ochid concept
  itself is dual-only (it points across repos), but the
  command-level error message offers no por-aware
  guidance.
- Severity / category: *Feature gap* (medium) +
  *Defaulting* (the commands silently assume dual).
- Equalization sketch: The bot thinks the natural shape
  is: detect por topology at command entry, and either
  no-op with a clear message ("por has no other repo;
  validate-desc has nothing to check") or refuse with a
  topology-aware error pointing at `--por`-equivalent
  workflows. The ochid concept stays dual-only.

## 8. Tests and fixtures

- Files touched:
  - `src/test_helpers.rs:68-150` (`Fixture` — dual),
    `153-220` (`FixturePor`).
  - `src/init/tests.rs` — 9 uses of `Fixture::new`, 5
    uses of `FixturePor::new` /
    `FixturePor::new_with_config` (clear por coverage
    here).
  - `src/push/integration_tests.rs` — 8 uses, **all
    `Fixture::new`**.
  - `src/sync/integration_tests.rs` — 9 uses, **all
    `Fixture::new`**.
  - `src/clone.rs` tests (228-303) — parse-only, no
    end-to-end por fixture exercising `clone_one`.
- Divergence: `FixturePor` exists and is exercised inside
  `init` tests, but no other integration-test surface
  drives a por workspace end-to-end. `push` and `sync`
  integration coverage is dual-only.
- Severity / category: *Coverage gap* (medium). `push`
  has no por tests because it has no por code path
  (compounding); `sync` *does* have a por code path
  (cwd-only resolution) but no fixture asserts it.
- Equalization sketch: The bot thinks `FixturePor` is the
  right starting point — extend it (e.g. add a
  `with_pending`-style variant) and add at least one
  `sync` integration test exercising `default_scope` →
  `Scope([Code])` against a `FixturePor`. `push` por
  coverage waits on push itself growing a por path.

## Summary

| Area | Category | Severity | Equalization size |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1. `PorFlag` reach | Defaulting + Feature gap | High | L |
| 2. `init` orchestration | Defaulting | Medium | M |
| 3. `clone` orchestration | Defaulting | Medium | M |
| 4. `push` — no por path | Feature gap | High | L |
| 5. `sync` — partial | Defaulting | Low | S |
| 6. `finalize` body | Coverage gap | Low | S |
| 7. ochid helpers / `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` | Feature gap + Defaulting | Medium | M |
| 8. Test fixtures | Coverage gap | Medium | S–M |

Headline: `push` is the largest gap (dual is structurally
baked in — there is no por code path at all), followed by
the runtime ochid helpers (`validate-desc` / `fix-desc`
error out instead of recognizing por). `sync` already
handles por cleanly via `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`
and is the closest model for "topology from config, not
from a flag." `init` / `clone` are symmetric at the
orchestrator layer but asymmetric at the defaulting layer
(dual is the implicit default, `--por` is the opt-out;
`--config` is por-only). Test coverage mirrors the code
gap: `FixturePor` exists but only `init` exercises it.

## Commonality

300	## Commonality
301	
302	The audit above inventoried *divergences*. This section
303	inverts the view — for each subcommand, what's already
304	shared between por and dual, what's dual-only, what's
305	por-only. Equalization is cheapest where the shared bucket
306	is already large and the dual-only bucket is a thin veneer.
307	
308	### Per-subcommand buckets
309	
310	#### `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — fully shared
311	
312	- **Shared (all)** — these four route through the
313	  `CommonArgs` + `common::for_each_repo(&c.repos, ...)`
314	  shape (`src/chid.rs:53`, `src/desc.rs:68`,
315	  `src/show.rs:118`, `src/list.rs`). Repos resolve via
316	  `default_scope` + `scope_to_repos` (`src/common.rs:594`,
317	  `:618`), which already handle por gracefully
318	  (`Scope([Code])` when `other-repo` is missing/empty).
319	- **Dual-only** — none in the runtime body. The dual case
320	  just adds a second entry to the repo list.
321	- **Por-only** — none.
322	
323	The bot thinks this family is the *template* for the rest
324	of the codebase. Scope-driven iteration is the working
325	model of topology-neutral dispatch.
326	
327	#### `sync` — fully shared
328	
329	- **Shared (all)** — same scope-driven shape as the
330	  `CommonArgs` family; per the 0.54.0 cleanup
331	  (`notes/todo.md > ## Done`) sync got `-R` and routes
332	  through `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`.
333	- **Dual-only** — none.
334	- **Por-only** — none.
335	
336	#### `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` — dual-only outliers
337	
338	- **Shared** — title-matching, ochid extraction, and the
339	  per-commit walk (`src/desc_helpers.rs` exports
340	  `extract_bare_id`, `find_matching_commit`,
341	  `validate_ochid`, etc. and these are topology-neutral).
342	- **Dual-only** — the entry shape. Both call
343	  `other_repo_from_config(&config)?` directly
344	  (`validate_desc.rs:133`, `fix_desc.rs:152`), which errors
345	  on por instead of resolving to a no-op or single-repo
346	  validation.
347	- **Por-only** — none.
348	
349	The bot thinks equalization here is local: convert the
350	dual-required prelude into a `default_scope`-style
351	resolution that no-ops `Side::Bot` when absent. The shared
352	body doesn't need changes.
353	
354	#### `init` / `clone` — shared body, dual default
355	
356	- **Shared** — repo-creation primitives (`init_one` /
357	  `clone_one` per `chores-07.md > init + clone redesign
358	  (0.41.1)`), GitHub remote provisioning, push retries,
359	  template seeding mechanism, `--account` / `--repo`
360	  resolution via `config.rs`.
361	- **Dual-only** — the orchestration outer loop runs twice
362	  (code + bot) when `--por` is absent; `.vc-config.toml`
363	  write; `.claude/` directory creation; `--use-template
364	  <code,bot>` accepts a bot value.
365	- **Por-only** — `--config <none|PATH>` (overrides the
366	  canned `.vc-config.toml` write, only meaningful when
367	  there'd be one).
368	
369	The asymmetry is at the *defaulting* layer (dual is the
370	implicit default; `--por` is the opt-out) more than the
371	*code* layer. The body is already roughly symmetric.
372	
373	#### `push` — dual-only
374	
375	- **Shared** — bookmark tracking, the push state machine's
376	  generic stages (`prepare`, `commit-app`, `push-app`),
377	  the retry/resume scaffolding around `.vc-x1/push-state.toml`.
378	- **Dual-only** — `claude_path()` resolution; the
379	  `CommitClaude` / `FinalizeClaude` stages; ochid trailer
380	  composition (`ochid: /.claude/<chid>` on app, multi-line
381	  `ochid: /<code-chid>` on bot); the `--from
382	  bookmark-both` flag; the 1:1 symmetric WC-commits
383	  assumption flagged in T1.
384	- **Por-only** — none.
385	
386	The largest gap. The bot thinks no por code path exists
387	*at all* today; a por workspace running `vc-x1 push` would
388	resolve nonexistent `.claude/` paths during the
389	`CommitClaude` stage.
390	
391	#### `finalize` — body shared, today's only use site is `push`
392	
393	- **Shared (all)** — the body is a single-repo operator
394	  (squash + commit-with-title-body + push). Topology-
395	  neutral. Standalone use on either code or bot is
396	  viable today (a user manually squashing a cycle into
397	  one commit and pushing) — see the matrix's
398	  topology-column note. `--scope=code` / `--scope=bot`
399	  selects the side at runtime.
400	- **Dual-only** — none in the body. The dual shape is
401	  *external*: `push` schedules a detached `finalize`
402	  against `.claude` only after `push-app` succeeds.
403	  `--detach` and `--delay` exist for that scheduled
404	  flow; standalone use ignores them.
405	- **Por-only** — none.
406	
407	Once `push` gains a por path, `finalize` requires no
408	changes. The standalone use case (T runtime + SC) is
409	already supported by the body; nothing new needed.
410	
411	#### Tests and fixtures
412	
413	- **Shared** — `Fixture` / `CliFixture` provide a dual
414	  workspace; `FixturePor` provides a por workspace; both
415	  honor `$VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR` / `$VC_X1_TEST_KEEP` and the
416	  RAII drop pattern.
417	- **Dual-only** — every integration test for `push` (8
418	  uses) and `sync` (9 uses); the `chid` / `desc` / `show` /
419	  `list` test paths (these route through `CommonArgs`,
420	  which works on por, but the fixtures don't cover that
421	  case).
422	- **Por-only** — five uses in `init/tests.rs` exercising
423	  the `--por` initialization path.
424	
425	The bot thinks the test-coverage gap mirrors the runtime
426	gap exactly: `push` and the desc outliers have zero por
427	coverage because the runtime code doesn't support por
428	there; the `CommonArgs` family has zero por coverage
429	despite supporting it.
430	
431	### Equalization candidates, ranked
432	
433	Ordered from closest-to-shared (cheap wins) to
434	furthest (architectural work):
435	
436	1. **`validate-desc` / `fix-desc`** — local refactor:
437	   replace the `other_repo_from_config` prelude with a
438	   scope-aware resolution that no-ops when `Side::Bot`
439	   is absent. Body unchanged. The bot thinks this is the
440	   smallest concrete equalization and a good prototype.
441	2. **`CommonArgs`-family por test coverage** — pure test
442	   work; surface bugs in scope handling without
443	   architectural risk. Likely lands a `FixturePor` use
444	   in `chid`/`desc`/`show`/`list` test modules.
445	3. **`init` / `clone` defaulting** — make `--por` /
446	   `--dual` peer flags rather than dual-default + `--por`
447	   opt-out; thread the chosen topology through the body
448	   without changing the inner primitives. User-config
449	   `[default].topology` (the original user proposal)
450	   becomes a small follow-on once peers exist.
451	4. **`push`** — the structural one. `claude_path()`,
452	   stage names, ochid composition all carry the
453	   dual-shape baked in. The bot thinks this is best done
454	   *after* `--por` becomes a runtime-known fact across
455	   all subcommands (i.e. after the desc outliers and
456	   defaulting are handled), so push has a stable contract
457	   to dispatch against.
458	5. **Topology-from-config rule** — codify (CLAUDE.md or a
459	   small ARCHITECTURE note) that every runtime subcommand
460	   resolves topology via `default_scope`, never from a
461	   `--por` flag. The flag stays creation-time only. The
462	   bot thinks this rule is the right *outcome* of the
463	   equalization above, not a prerequisite — codify it
464	   once the prototype validates the shape.
465	
466	### Summary
467	
468	Three structural classes today:
469	
470	- **Topology-neutral via scope** —
471	  `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` / `sync` / `finalize`-body.
472	  This is the working pattern.
473	- **Topology-required (dual)** —
474	  `push` / `validate-desc` / `fix-desc`. These bypass
475	  scope and assume `.claude/` exists.
476	- **Topology-creating** — `init` / `clone`. The `--por`
477	  flag's only legitimate home; chooses the workspace
478	  shape that downstream commands then read from
479	  `.vc-config.toml`.
480	
481	Equalization is "make Topology-required match
482	Topology-neutral via scope" — not a new pattern, just
483	extending an existing one. The smallest concrete win is
484	`validate-desc` / `fix-desc`; the largest is `push`. The
485	two creation-time commands stay roughly as-is, with the
486	defaulting layer (peer flags + optional user-config
487	default) as the only change.
488	
489	## Feature axes
490	
491	The audit and commonality passes treat `--por` and `--dual`
492	as the primitives. They aren't — they're bundles. The
493	concrete shape `vc-x1 init --por <name>` produces today is
494	"single repo, with a (degenerate) `.vc-config.toml`, pushed
495	to a freshly-created GitHub repo." Each of those is an
496	independent choice, but the user can only ask for them as a
497	bundle.
498	
499	This section names the independent axes, says what each
500	controls, lists today's flags and defaults, and identifies
501	gaps. **No implementation — defining only.** Equalization
502	across these axes lands in 0.62.0+ cycles.
503	
504	### Axes
505	
506	#### Topology
507	
508	- **States** — `single` (one code repo) | `dual` (code repo
509	  + `.claude/` companion cross-linked by `ochid:`).
510	- **Today** — dual is the implicit default; `--por`
511	  is the boolean opt-out. No `--dual` peer.
512	- **Surface** — `--por` (boolean, `init` and `clone` only).
513	  Downstream commands infer from `.vc-config.toml >
514	  [workspace] other-repo` via `default_scope`.
515	- **Gap** — `--dual` doesn't exist as a peer flag, so the
516	  defaulting is asymmetric: you can be explicit about por
517	  but not about dual.
518	
519	**Decisions (0.61.0):**
520	
521	- **Add `--dual` peer flag**. `--por` / `--dual`
522	  exactly-one-of (clap-enforced). Internal value is an
523	  enum, not a boolean — eliminates the `--por`/`!--por`
524	  framing that the audit found everywhere.
525	- **Default: `dual`** (user reversed from an earlier
526	  strict-explicit-required position once the broader
527	  "defaults where natural" principle landed). Dual is
528	  the primary use case; defaulting to it matches
529	  reality and minimizes new-user friction.
530	- **Runtime override on every subcommand** (not just
531	  `init` / `clone`). `--por` at runtime short-circuits
532	  `default_scope` → `Scope([Code])` regardless of what
533	  the workspace says. Escape hatch for "this command,
534	  just my code, leave `.claude/` alone."
535	- **Workspace `.vc-config.toml` is dual-only**. Por
536	  never reads, never creates. Today's degenerate
537	  `[workspace] path = "/"` write under `--por` gets
538	  dropped.
539	- **Resolution chain**: CLI > ENV > Local > Global >
540	  baked-in default (`dual`). No "Error" floor for
541	  Topology — baked-in always supplies.
542	
543	#### `.vc-config.toml` write — collapsed
544	
545	This axis was originally written as independent. After
546	the Topology decisions, **it collapses** — presence of
547	the workspace `.vc-config.toml` is perfectly correlated
548	with Topology's choice:
549	
550	- Topology=por → no `.vc-config.toml` (por never reads
551	  or writes the workspace file).
552	- Topology=dual → `.vc-config.toml` is mandatory (it's
553	  what detects dual at runtime).
554	
555	This is **not an independent axis**.
556	
557	The capability `--config <path>` was meant to serve
558	(custom workspace metadata, arbitrary file copy) is
559	deferred to a broader **copying** design — see
560	[`notes/design-cli/copying.md`](copying.md) [[3]]. That design uses
561	`--init-from-code` / `--init-from-bot` flags to copy
562	arbitrary files (including `.vc-config.toml` and
563	`.gitignore`), suppresses canned writes when engaged,
564	and defers the "is this dual workspace functional?"
565	check to the first downstream subcommand.
566	
567	Today's `--config <none|PATH>` flag (`init.rs:610`,
568	"only valid with `--por`") becomes vestigial under the
569	new design. The 0.62.0+ rollout drops it.
570	
571	**Decisions (0.61.0):**
572	
573	- **Collapse this axis**. Not independent; presence-of-
574	  `.vc-config.toml` correlates 1:1 with Topology.
575	- **Drop `--config <path>`** from the surface; its
576	  capability moves under `--init-from*`.
577	- **Drop from the axis list at close-out**.
578	
579	#### Remote provisioning
580	
581	- **States** — `github-create` (default; creates
582	  `winksaville/<name>` via gh CLI) | `github-skip` (URL
583	  is non-GitHub or pre-existing) | `local-bare` (`git
584	  init --bare` under `--repo local=<dir>`) | `none`
585	  (no remote configured at all — just the working repo).
586	- **Today** — `--repo <cat>[=<val>]` resolves to one of
587	  the first three via the user-config `[account.<a>.
588	  repo.category]` lookup; `none` doesn't exist.
589	- **Surface** — `--repo` for the cat/val, `--private` for
590	  GitHub visibility, `--push-retries` /
591	  `--push-retry-delay` for the post-create push.
592	- **Gap** — no flag for "no remote." A user wanting a
593	  local workspace (or to provision the remote separately)
594	  has no clean way to express it.
595	
596	**Decisions (0.61.0):**
597	
598	- **Add `none` as a third peer category**. Spelled
599	  `--repo none` (no value). User-config equivalent:
600	  `[account.<a>].repo.default = "none"` or top-level
601	  `[repo].default = "none"`. Three peer categories now:
602	  `remote` / `local` / `none`. No special-casing.
603	- **`none` is first-class** in the resolution chain.
604	  Same `--repo` CLI > ENV > Local > Global > default
605	  resolution path as the other categories.
606	- **When `--repo none`, `--account` is skipped**.
607	  Account is meaningless without a remote provider to
608	  consult. The error floor only applies to keys *needed*
609	  for the requested operation.
610	- **`--private` errors when category != github-ish**
611	  (not silent-ignore). Surfaces footgun cases like
612	  `--repo local=... --private`.
613	- **Uniform across dual sides**. `--repo` applies to
614	  both code and `.claude/` companion (today's behavior).
615	  Per-side `--repo-code` / `--repo-bot` deferred to a
616	  future cycle if a real need surfaces.
617	- **Remote-create-fail recovery flag** (`--remote-
618	  provisioned` or similar) deferred to a follow-up
619	  cycle. Today's abort-on-failure is acceptable.
620	- **Default: `github-create`** (today's behavior;
621	  natural-default axis). Resolution chain ends in
622	  baked-in default for the category; account / URL
623	  prefix still error at Global if unset (user-specific
624	  keys, no natural default possible).
625	
626	#### Privacy
627	
628	- **States** — `public` (default) | `private`.
629	- **Today** — `--private` flag.
630	- **Surface** — `--private`.
631	- **Scope** — only meaningful when Remote = `github-create`.
632	- **Gap** — none structurally; the flag is independent
633	  and orthogonal.
634	
635	**Decisions (0.61.0):**
636	
637	- **Default: `public`** (preserves today's behavior).
638	  User explicitly chose default-public over both
639	  strict-explicit-required and default-private; rationale
640	  is the user's personal context (no paid private plan)
641	  and back-compat. Consistent with the broader "defaults
642	  where natural" principle.
643	- **Add `--public` peer flag**. `--private` / `--public`
644	  exactly-one-of. Needed because `[default].private =
645	  true` is settable in user-config, so a per-invocation
646	  spelling for "public" must exist. Same shape as `--por`
647	  / `--dual`.
648	- **Errors when category != github-ish** (per Remote
649	  decision; no silent ignore).
650	- **Resolution chain**: CLI > ENV > Local > Global >
651	  baked-in default (`public`). No error floor.
652	
653	#### Copying (today: `--use-template`; designed: `--init-from*`)
654	
655	- **States** — `none` (default) | `code-only <path>` |
656	  `code-and-bot <path,path>`.
657	- **Today** — `--use-template <CODE[,BOT]>`. Second value
658	  is dual-only.
659	- **Surface** — `--use-template`.
660	- **Gap** — none structurally; per-side templating is
661	  naturally Topology-aware. Bot-side template under
662	  Topology=single is meaningless and rejected today.
663	
664	**Decisions (0.61.0):**
665	
666	This axis is renamed **Copying** and significantly
667	expanded in scope — see [`notes/design-cli/copying.md`](copying.md) [[3]].
668	The decisions below are the axis-level summary;
669	behavior details live in the design stub.
670	
671	- **Surface**: `--init-from-code` / `--init-from-bot` /
672	  `--init-from` (shorthand for `-code`) and their
673	  `-recursive` variants. Each accepts a shell glob;
674	  each may be specified multiple times (additive).
675	- **Subsumes** `--use-template`, `--config <path>`, and
676	  the hypothetical `--gitignore <path>`.
677	- **Pure file copy**. No variable substitution
678	  (deliberately — that's a different feature).
679	- **Last-writer-wins + warning on collision** when
680	  multiple sources resolve to the same destination.
681	- **Canned writes suppressed when copying engaged**.
682	  Any `--init-from*` present → `init` skips the canned
683	  `.vc-config.toml` + `.gitignore` writes entirely.
684	- **Deferred dual validation**. Missing `.vc-config.toml`
685	  after copy is a warning at `init`; first downstream
686	  subcommand errors. Lets users supply config post-init.
687	- **Fixed filename**. `.vc-config.toml` is the only
688	  workspace-config name; overriding is deferred.
689	- **Symlink + exec-bit preservation** (`cp -a`
690	  semantics).
691	- **Source-not-found = hard error** (typo protection).
692	  Empty glob expansion is fine.
693	- **Config-pinnable** as a list-of-strings:
694	  `[default].init-from = ["~/tpl/*", "~/tpl/.gitignore"]`.
695	  Requires a small list-of-strings extension to the
696	  config parser.
697	- **CLI overrides config entirely** (no union). If user
698	  passes any `--init-from*` on CLI, the config-pinned
699	  list is ignored — match the broader chain rule.

## Commonality

The audit above inventoried *divergences*. This section
inverts the view — for each subcommand, what's already
shared between por and dual, what's dual-only, what's
por-only. Equalization is cheapest where the shared bucket
is already large and the dual-only bucket is a thin veneer.

### Per-subcommand buckets

#### `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — fully shared

- **Shared (all)** — these four route through the
  `CommonArgs` + `common::for_each_repo(&c.repos, ...)`
  shape (`src/chid.rs:53`, `src/desc.rs:68`,
  `src/show.rs:118`, `src/list.rs`). Repos resolve via
  `default_scope` + `scope_to_repos` (`src/common.rs:594`,
  `:618`), which already handle por gracefully
  (`Scope([Code])` when `other-repo` is missing/empty).
- **Dual-only** — none in the runtime body. The dual case
  just adds a second entry to the repo list.
- **Por-only** — none.

The bot thinks this family is the *template* for the rest
of the codebase. Scope-driven iteration is the working
model of topology-neutral dispatch.

#### `sync` — fully shared

- **Shared (all)** — same scope-driven shape as the
  `CommonArgs` family; per the 0.54.0 cleanup
  (`notes/todo.md > ## Done`) sync got `-R` and routes
  through `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`.
- **Dual-only** — none.
- **Por-only** — none.

#### `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` — dual-only outliers

- **Shared** — title-matching, ochid extraction, and the
  per-commit walk (`src/desc_helpers.rs` exports
  `extract_bare_id`, `find_matching_commit`,
  `validate_ochid`, etc. and these are topology-neutral).
- **Dual-only** — the entry shape. Both call
  `other_repo_from_config(&config)?` directly
  (`validate_desc.rs:133`, `fix_desc.rs:152`), which errors
  on por instead of resolving to a no-op or single-repo
  validation.
- **Por-only** — none.

The bot thinks equalization here is local: convert the
dual-required prelude into a `default_scope`-style
resolution that no-ops `Side::Bot` when absent. The shared
body doesn't need changes.

#### `init` / `clone` — shared body, dual default

- **Shared** — repo-creation primitives (`init_one` /
  `clone_one` per `chores-07.md > init + clone redesign
  (0.41.1)`), GitHub remote provisioning, push retries,
  template seeding mechanism, `--account` / `--repo`
  resolution via `config.rs`.
- **Dual-only** — the orchestration outer loop runs twice
  (code + bot) when `--por` is absent; `.vc-config.toml`
  write; `.claude/` directory creation; `--use-template
  <code,bot>` accepts a bot value.
- **Por-only** — `--config <none|PATH>` (overrides the
  canned `.vc-config.toml` write, only meaningful when
  there'd be one).

The asymmetry is at the *defaulting* layer (dual is the
implicit default; `--por` is the opt-out) more than the
*code* layer. The body is already roughly symmetric.

#### `push` — dual-only

- **Shared** — bookmark tracking, the push state machine's
  generic stages (`prepare`, `commit-app`, `push-app`),
  the retry/resume scaffolding around `.vc-x1/push-state.toml`.
- **Dual-only** — `claude_path()` resolution; the
  `CommitClaude` / `FinalizeClaude` stages; ochid trailer
  composition (`ochid: /.claude/<chid>` on app, multi-line
  `ochid: /<code-chid>` on bot); the `--from
  bookmark-both` flag; the 1:1 symmetric WC-commits
  assumption flagged in T1.
- **Por-only** — none.

The largest gap. The bot thinks no por code path exists
*at all* today; a por workspace running `vc-x1 push` would
resolve nonexistent `.claude/` paths during the
`CommitClaude` stage.

#### `finalize` — body shared, today's only use site is `push`

- **Shared (all)** — the body is a single-repo operator
  (squash + commit-with-title-body + push). Topology-
  neutral. Standalone use on either code or bot is
  viable today (a user manually squashing a cycle into
  one commit and pushing) — see the matrix's
  topology-column note. `--scope=code` / `--scope=bot`
  selects the side at runtime.
- **Dual-only** — none in the body. The dual shape is
  *external*: `push` schedules a detached `finalize`
  against `.claude` only after `push-app` succeeds.
  `--detach` and `--delay` exist for that scheduled
  flow; standalone use ignores them.
- **Por-only** — none.

Once `push` gains a por path, `finalize` requires no
changes. The standalone use case (T runtime + SC) is
already supported by the body; nothing new needed.

#### Tests and fixtures

- **Shared** — `Fixture` / `CliFixture` provide a dual
  workspace; `FixturePor` provides a por workspace; both
  honor `$VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR` / `$VC_X1_TEST_KEEP` and the
  RAII drop pattern.
- **Dual-only** — every integration test for `push` (8
  uses) and `sync` (9 uses); the `chid` / `desc` / `show` /
  `list` test paths (these route through `CommonArgs`,
  which works on por, but the fixtures don't cover that
  case).
- **Por-only** — five uses in `init/tests.rs` exercising
  the `--por` initialization path.

The bot thinks the test-coverage gap mirrors the runtime
gap exactly: `push` and the desc outliers have zero por
coverage because the runtime code doesn't support por
there; the `CommonArgs` family has zero por coverage
despite supporting it.

### Equalization candidates, ranked

Ordered from closest-to-shared (cheap wins) to
furthest (architectural work):

1. **`validate-desc` / `fix-desc`** — local refactor:
   replace the `other_repo_from_config` prelude with a
   scope-aware resolution that no-ops when `Side::Bot`
   is absent. Body unchanged. The bot thinks this is the
   smallest concrete equalization and a good prototype.
2. **`CommonArgs`-family por test coverage** — pure test
   work; surface bugs in scope handling without
   architectural risk. Likely lands a `FixturePor` use
   in `chid`/`desc`/`show`/`list` test modules.
3. **`init` / `clone` defaulting** — make `--por` /
   `--dual` peer flags rather than dual-default + `--por`
   opt-out; thread the chosen topology through the body
   without changing the inner primitives. User-config
   `[default].topology` (the original user proposal)
   becomes a small follow-on once peers exist.
4. **`push`** — the structural one. `claude_path()`,
   stage names, ochid composition all carry the
   dual-shape baked in. The bot thinks this is best done
   *after* `--por` becomes a runtime-known fact across
   all subcommands (i.e. after the desc outliers and
   defaulting are handled), so push has a stable contract
   to dispatch against.
5. **Topology-from-config rule** — codify (CLAUDE.md or a
   small ARCHITECTURE note) that every runtime subcommand
   resolves topology via `default_scope`, never from a
   `--por` flag. The flag stays creation-time only. The
   bot thinks this rule is the right *outcome* of the
   equalization above, not a prerequisite — codify it
   once the prototype validates the shape.

### Summary

Three structural classes today:

- **Topology-neutral via scope** —
  `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` / `sync` / `finalize`-body.
  This is the working pattern.
- **Topology-required (dual)** —
  `push` / `validate-desc` / `fix-desc`. These bypass
  scope and assume `.claude/` exists.
- **Topology-creating** — `init` / `clone`. The `--por`
  flag's only legitimate home; chooses the workspace
  shape that downstream commands then read from
  `.vc-config.toml`.

Equalization is "make Topology-required match
Topology-neutral via scope" — not a new pattern, just
extending an existing one. The smallest concrete win is
`validate-desc` / `fix-desc`; the largest is `push`. The
two creation-time commands stay roughly as-is, with the
defaulting layer (peer flags + optional user-config
default) as the only change.

  both code and `.claude/` companion (today's behavior).
  Per-side `--repo-code` / `--repo-bot` deferred to a
  future cycle if a real need surfaces.
- **Remote-create-fail recovery flag** (`--remote-
  provisioned` or similar) deferred to a follow-up
  cycle. Today's abort-on-failure is acceptable.
- **Default: `github-create`** (today's behavior;
  natural-default axis). Resolution chain ends in
  baked-in default for the category; account / URL
  prefix still error at Global if unset (user-specific
  keys, no natural default possible).

#### Privacy

- **States** — `public` (default) | `private`.
- **Today** — `--private` flag.
- **Surface** — `--private`.
- **Scope** — only meaningful when Remote = `github-create`.
- **Gap** — none structurally; the flag is independent
  and orthogonal.

**Decisions (0.61.0):**

- **Default: `public`** (preserves today's behavior).
  User explicitly chose default-public over both
  strict-explicit-required and default-private; rationale
  is the user's personal context (no paid private plan)
  and back-compat. Consistent with the broader "defaults
  where natural" principle.
- **Add `--public` peer flag**. `--private` / `--public`
  exactly-one-of. Needed because `[default].private =
  true` is settable in user-config, so a per-invocation
  spelling for "public" must exist. Same shape as `--por`
  / `--dual`.
- **Errors when category != github-ish** (per Remote
  decision; no silent ignore).
- **Resolution chain**: CLI > ENV > Local > Global >
  baked-in default (`public`). No error floor.

#### Copying (today: `--use-template`; designed: `--init-from*`)

- **States** — `none` (default) | `code-only <path>` |
  `code-and-bot <path,path>`.
- **Today** — `--use-template <CODE[,BOT]>`. Second value
  is dual-only.
- **Surface** — `--use-template`.
- **Gap** — none structurally; per-side templating is
  naturally Topology-aware. Bot-side template under
  Topology=single is meaningless and rejected today.

**Decisions (0.61.0):**

This axis is renamed **Copying** and significantly
expanded in scope — see [`notes/design-cli/copying.md`](copying.md) [[3]].
The decisions below are the axis-level summary;
behavior details live in the design stub.

- **Surface**: `--init-from-code` / `--init-from-bot` /
  `--init-from` (shorthand for `-code`) and their
  `-recursive` variants. Each accepts a shell glob;
  each may be specified multiple times (additive).
- **Subsumes** `--use-template`, `--config <path>`, and
  the hypothetical `--gitignore <path>`.
- **Pure file copy**. No variable substitution
  (deliberately — that's a different feature).
- **Last-writer-wins + warning on collision** when
  multiple sources resolve to the same destination.
- **Canned writes suppressed when copying engaged**.
  Any `--init-from*` present → `init` skips the canned
  `.vc-config.toml` + `.gitignore` writes entirely.
- **Deferred dual validation**. Missing `.vc-config.toml`
  after copy is a warning at `init`; first downstream
  subcommand errors. Lets users supply config post-init.
- **Fixed filename**. `.vc-config.toml` is the only
  workspace-config name; overriding is deferred.
- **Symlink + exec-bit preservation** (`cp -a`
  semantics).
- **Source-not-found = hard error** (typo protection).
  Empty glob expansion is fine.
- **Config-pinnable** as a list-of-strings:
  `[default].init-from = ["~/tpl/*", "~/tpl/.gitignore"]`.
  Requires a small list-of-strings extension to the
  config parser.
- **CLI overrides config entirely** (no union). If user
  passes any `--init-from*` on CLI, the config-pinned
  list is ignored — match the broader chain rule.

737	### Mapping `--por` / `--dual` onto axis combinations
738	
739	Today `--por` and (implicit) `--dual` are *bundle
740	shorthands*. After axes are independent, they remain as
741	shorthands for the common combinations:
742	
743	- `--dual` (or no flag — back-compat) →

745	  create, Privacy=public, Copying=none)`.

747	  Remote=github-create, Privacy=public, Copying=none)`.
748	  Note `Vc-config=written` is what today's `--por`
749	  actually does, not `not-written` (the `.vc-config.toml`
750	  is still written, just degenerate).

752	A user wanting a fully-plain single repo today can't
753	spell it; tomorrow it would be `--por --config none --no-remote` (or
754	shorthand `--bare` if that combination is common enough to
755	deserve one).

757	### Connections to user-config
758	
759	Once axes are independent, each maps to a `[default].*`
760	key in `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`, so the user-config
761	proposal that opened this cycle lands cleanly:
762	
763	- `[default].topology = "single" | "dual"`
764	- `[default].write-vc-config = true | false | "<path>"`
765	- `[default].remote = "github" | "local" | "none"`
766	- `[default].private = true | false`
767	
768	`--account`-scoped overrides ride on the existing
769	`[account.<a>]` substrate. The bot thinks Remote is the
770	most useful default to make user-configurable (different
771	accounts → different remote providers), with Topology
772	second.
773	
774	### Resolution chain
775	
776	The per-axis sections above each say "CLI → user-config →
777	default (or error)." That's a sketch; the full resolution
778	chain has **five layers**, with the bottom varying per
779	axis. The same chain applies to **every axis** and every
780	field a config carries (account, repo category/value,
781	privacy, topology, copying sources, …) — nothing axis-
782	specific.
783	
784	#### Layers (highest precedence first)
785	
786	1. **CLI flag** — `--por`, `--account`, `--private`,
787	   `--config <path>`, `--global-config <path>`, etc. The
788	   per-invocation surface. CLI is god — wins over every
789	   other layer when present. CLI carries *values* only;
790	   there are **no per-axis "default-X" flags** (to change
791	   a default, edit a config — local, global, or the
792	   baked-in starting point).
793	2. **Environment variable** — `VC_X1_<KEY>`. The
794	   per-session surface, set via `export` or one-shot
795	   prefix. Loses to CLI.
796	3. **Local config** — `./.vc-config.toml` (the workspace
797	   metadata file), or whatever `--config <path>` resolved
798	   to. Per-project durable intent. Loses to env-var and
799	   CLI; **may carry any field a CLI flag exposes** — no
800	   carve-outs (a local config can pin
801	   `[global] config-path` just as it pins `account`).
802	4. **Global config** — `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`
803	   (XDG-aware), or whatever `--global-config <path>` /
804	   `VC_X1_GLOBAL_CONFIG` / a local-config
805	   `[global] config-path` resolved to. Per-user durable
806	   defaults. Lowest user-editable layer.
807	5. **Baked-in default config** — a `default-config.toml`
808	   compiled into the binary via `include_str!`. Acts as
809	   the bottom-of-chain fallback for axes with natural
810	   defaults. The user can extract it with **`vc-x1
811	   config dump`** (a new subcommand), modify, and save
812	   as their global config or as a local override. Future:
813	   `linkme`-style registry could ship multiple baked-in
814	   profiles selectable at install or post-install
815	   (deferred).
816	
817	Each user-facing layer (1–4) is optional. Layer 5
818	(baked-in) is always present.
819	
820	#### Two-class resolution (per axis)
821	
822	Not every key has a natural default. The chain resolves
823	differently for the two classes:
824	
825	- **Natural-default keys** — Topology, Privacy, Remote
826	  category, Copying. The baked-in supplies a default
827	  (`dual`, `public`, `github-create`, `none`). The chain
828	  effectively ends at layer 5, never at "error" — naked
829	  `vc-x1 init foo` with no user config works.
830	- **User-specific keys** — `[default].account`, account
831	  repo URLs (`[account.<a>].repo.category.remote = "..."`),
832	  account repo fixtures. The baked-in cannot supply these
833	  — there's no universal `git@github.com:<your-handle>`
834	  to bake in. The chain ends at "Error" if layers 1–4
835	  don't supply: *"no `<key>` specified; set it in
836	  `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` or via the appropriate
837	  CLI flag."*
838	
839	The principle is **"defaults where natural; errors only
840	for user-specific keys"** — it matches what `resolve_repo`
841	already does today for `--account` / `--repo` (errors
842	specifically when those keys are missing, not when any
843	key is missing).

848	  invocation. Today functionally equivalent to runtime
849	  `--por` (because local config carries only topology
850	  metadata); add as a peer flag once local config gains
851	  non-topology fields.
852	- **`--no-global-config`** — skip layer 4 entirely.
853	  Necessary for tests / CI / "ignore my defaults this

856	Both flags are layer-1 (CLI) — they can't themselves be

861	Both local and global config can redirect to a different
862	config file via `[global] config-path = "…"` (in either

| Axis | Default | Today's flag | Independent today? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Topology | dual | `--por` (opt-out only) | No (no `--dual` peer) |
| `.vc-config.toml` write | written | `--config <none\|PATH>` | No (rejected when dual) |
| Remote | github-create | `--repo <cat>[=<val>]` | Mostly (`none` missing) |
| Privacy | public | `--private` | Yes |
| Copying | none | `--use-template <C[,B]>` | Yes (Topology-aware) |
| Scaffolding | on | (none) | Not an axis today |

### Mapping `--por` / `--dual` onto axis combinations

Today `--por` and (implicit) `--dual` are *bundle
shorthands*. After axes are independent, they remain as
shorthands for the common combinations:

- `--dual` (or no flag — back-compat) →
  `(Topology=dual, Vc-config=written, Remote=github-
  create, Privacy=public, Copying=none)`.
- `--por` → `(Topology=single, Vc-config=written,
  Remote=github-create, Privacy=public, Copying=none)`.
  Note `Vc-config=written` is what today's `--por`
  actually does, not `not-written` (the `.vc-config.toml`
  is still written, just degenerate).

A user wanting a fully-plain single repo today can't
spell it; tomorrow it would be `--por --config none --no-remote` (or
shorthand `--bare` if that combination is common enough to
deserve one).

### Connections to user-config

Once axes are independent, each maps to a `[default].*`
key in `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`, so the user-config
proposal that opened this cycle lands cleanly:

- `[default].topology = "single" | "dual"`
- `[default].write-vc-config = true | false | "<path>"`
- `[default].remote = "github" | "local" | "none"`
- `[default].private = true | false`

`--account`-scoped overrides ride on the existing
`[account.<a>]` substrate. The bot thinks Remote is the
most useful default to make user-configurable (different
accounts → different remote providers), with Topology
second.

### Resolution chain

The per-axis sections above each say "CLI → user-config →
default (or error)." That's a sketch; the full resolution
chain has **five layers**, with the bottom varying per
axis. The same chain applies to **every axis** and every
field a config carries (account, repo category/value,
privacy, topology, copying sources, …) — nothing axis-
specific.

#### Layers (highest precedence first)

1. **CLI flag** — `--por`, `--account`, `--private`,
   `--config <path>`, `--global-config <path>`, etc. The
   per-invocation surface. CLI is god — wins over every
   other layer when present. CLI carries *values* only;
   there are **no per-axis "default-X" flags** (to change
   a default, edit a config — local, global, or the
   baked-in starting point).
2. **Environment variable** — `VC_X1_<KEY>`. The
   per-session surface, set via `export` or one-shot
   prefix. Loses to CLI.
3. **Local config** — `./.vc-config.toml` (the workspace
   metadata file), or whatever `--config <path>` resolved
   to. Per-project durable intent. Loses to env-var and
   CLI; **may carry any field a CLI flag exposes** — no
   carve-outs (a local config can pin
   `[global] config-path` just as it pins `account`).
4. **Global config** — `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`
   (XDG-aware), or whatever `--global-config <path>` /
   `VC_X1_GLOBAL_CONFIG` / a local-config
   `[global] config-path` resolved to. Per-user durable
   defaults. Lowest user-editable layer.
5. **Baked-in default config** — a `default-config.toml`
   compiled into the binary via `include_str!`. Acts as
   the bottom-of-chain fallback for axes with natural
   defaults. The user can extract it with **`vc-x1
   config dump`** (a new subcommand), modify, and save
   as their global config or as a local override. Future:
   `linkme`-style registry could ship multiple baked-in
   profiles selectable at install or post-install
   (deferred).

Each user-facing layer (1–4) is optional. Layer 5
(baked-in) is always present.

#### Two-class resolution (per axis)

Not every key has a natural default. The chain resolves
differently for the two classes:

- **Natural-default keys** — Topology, Privacy, Remote
  category, Copying. The baked-in supplies a default
  (`dual`, `public`, `github-create`, `none`). The chain
  effectively ends at layer 5, never at "error" — naked
  `vc-x1 init foo` with no user config works.
- **User-specific keys** — `[default].account`, account
  repo URLs (`[account.<a>].repo.category.remote = "..."`),
  account repo fixtures. The baked-in cannot supply these
  — there's no universal `git@github.com:<your-handle>`
  to bake in. The chain ends at "Error" if layers 1–4
  don't supply: *"no `<key>` specified; set it in
  `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` or via the appropriate
  CLI flag."*

The principle is **"defaults where natural; errors only
for user-specific keys"** — it matches what `resolve_repo`
already does today for `--account` / `--repo` (errors
specifically when those keys are missing, not when any
key is missing).

#### Escape hatches

- **`--no-local-config`** — skip layer 3 entirely for this
  invocation. Today functionally equivalent to runtime
  `--por` (because local config carries only topology
  metadata); add as a peer flag once local config gains
  non-topology fields.
- **`--no-global-config`** — skip layer 4 entirely.
  Necessary for tests / CI / "ignore my defaults this
  once."

Both flags are layer-1 (CLI) — they can't themselves be
pinned by config, since their purpose is to ignore config.

#### Redirection and cycles

Both local and global config can redirect to a different
config file via `[global] config-path = "…"` (in either
file) and `[local] config-path = "…"` (in global).
**Circular redirections are user error** — the loader
keeps a visited-set and errors with the cycle path; no
attempt is made to break the cycle automatically.

The bot thinks redirection in local pointing at a
custom global is the most useful case (a project that
ships a team-wide default file in the repo), and global
pointing at another global is mostly an unintended
consequence of letting the field exist universally.
Allowing it costs nothing.

#### Env-var naming

`VC_X1_<KEY>`, flat namespace, key matches the resolved
field name:

| Axis | Env-var | Resolves to |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Topology | `VC_X1_TOPOLOGY` | `single` \| `dual` |
| Remote (account) | `VC_X1_ACCOUNT` | account name |
| Remote (repo) | `VC_X1_REPO` | `<cat>` or `<cat>=<val>` |
| Privacy | `VC_X1_PRIVATE` | `true` \| `false` |
| Copying | `VC_X1_INIT_FROM*` | colon-separated globs |
| — | `VC_X1_CONFIG` | local config path or `none` |
| — | `VC_X1_GLOBAL_CONFIG` | global config path |
| — | `VC_X1_NO_LOCAL_CONFIG` | `true` \| `false` |
| — | `VC_X1_NO_GLOBAL_CONFIG` | `true` \| `false` |

Precedent in the codebase: `config.rs:113` already honors
`XDG_CONFIG_HOME`; `test_tmp_root.rs` uses
`VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR` / `VC_X1_TEST_KEEP`. The pattern is
established; `VC_X1_*` just extends it.

#### Por's view of the chain

Following the decisions in this cycle:

- **Workspace `.vc-config.toml`** (layer 3) — dual-only.
  Por never reads, never creates. Today's degenerate
  `[workspace] path = "/"` write under `--por` is residue
  from when `--por` was a half-implemented opt-out and
  should be dropped.
- **User-config `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`**
  (layer 4) — topology-neutral. Both por and dual consult
  it.
- **Runtime `--por`** — overrides workspace topology
  detection across every subcommand (not just
  `init`/`clone`). In a dual workspace, runtime `--por`
  short-circuits `default_scope` → `Scope([Code])` and
  ignores `.claude/`.

### Subcommand × parameter matrix

The designed-state at-a-glance view — for each
subcommand (rows), which parameter families it accepts
(columns), and a short note on what the parameter does
in that context.

This is the **post-equalization target**, not today's
state. Today many of these cells are gaps (per the audit
findings); the matrix captures what 0.62.0+ equalization
cycles drive toward.

**Ledger — column acronyms map to axes (or to
cross-cutting flag groups):**

| Acronym | Axis / group | Flags |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **T** | Topology | `--por` / `--dual` (creation on `init`/`clone`; runtime override on every other subcommand) |
| **A/R** | Remote | `--account` + `--repo` (remote provider resolution; user-config-keyed) |
| **Priv** | Privacy | `--private` / `--public` (GitHub visibility) |
| **CP** | Copying | `--init-from*` family (file copying into the workspace) |
| **CFG** | *Config paths* (not an axis) | `--config <path>` / `--global-config <path>` (point at non-default config files) |
| **NO-CFG** | *Config skip* (not an axis) | `--no-local-config` / `--no-global-config` (skip layers in the resolution chain) |
| **SC** | *Scope* (runtime selection, not an axis) | `--scope` / `-R` (per-invocation repo selection on dual workspaces) |

The first four acronyms match feature axes (negotiated
in this cycle); the last three are cross-cutting flag
groups that don't correspond to single axes but appear on
many subcommands.

**Cell legend:**

- `✓` — accepted; default behavior.
- `✓*` — accepted with caveat (see footnotes).
- `—` — not applicable for this subcommand.
- `(new)` — subcommand or capability added by the
  0.62.0+ rollout.

| Subcommand | T | A/R | Priv | CP | CFG | NO-CFG | SC |
| --- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| `init <TARGET>` | ✓ creation | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — |
| `clone <URL>` | ✓ creation | ✓ | ✓ | ✓* | ✓ | ✓ | — |
| `push <BM>` | ✓ runtime | — | — | — | ✓ | ✓ | ✓* |
| `sync` | ✓ runtime | — | — | — | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| `finalize` | ✓ runtime | — | — | — | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| `chid` | ✓ runtime | — | — | — | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| `desc` | ✓ runtime | — | — | — | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| `show` | ✓ runtime | — | — | — | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| `list` | ✓ runtime | — | — | — | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| `validate-desc` | ✓ runtime | — | — | — | ✓ | ✓ | ✓* |
| `fix-desc` | ✓ runtime | — | — | — | ✓ | ✓ | ✓* |
| `validate-todo` | — | — | — | — | ✓ | ✓ | — |
| `fix-todo` | — | — | — | — | ✓ | ✓ | — |
| `config dump` (new) | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |

**Footnotes:**

- `clone CP*` — copying on `clone` overlays *on top of*
  the cloned content (same flag set, same collision
  rules as `init`).
- `push SC*` — push uses bookmark scoping rather than
  `--scope`; the cell marks "yes, push reads scope-ish
  info" without implying surface identity.
- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `SC*` — these are the
  desc-family outliers that bypass `for_each_repo`
  today (per `## Commonality`); post-equalization
  they route through scope like the rest of the
  `CommonArgs` family.
- `config dump` is a new subcommand to be added — emits
  the baked-in default config so users can save and
  modify it.

**Topology column (`T`) — quick reference:**

- *Creation* (init/clone): `--por` / `--dual` chooses
  the workspace shape that gets written into
  `.vc-config.toml` (dual only — por writes nothing).
- *Runtime override* (other subcommands): `--por` at
  invocation short-circuits `default_scope` →
  `Scope([Code])` regardless of workspace contents.
  Default-dual still applies at the workspace level
  (the `.vc-config.toml` says dual, but the user is
  asking for "this command only, just my code").
- `finalize` accepts T as a runtime override and SC for
  per-side selection. Today's only use site is inside
  `push`'s workflow against a specific repo path, but
  the body itself is generic (squash + commit with
  title/body + push) and works on either side
  standalone — `--scope=code` or `--scope=bot`. Use
  case: a user manually squashing a cycle into one
  commit and pushing. `--squash` is logically optional
  (auto-detect from `@` being empty); `--detach` and
  `--delay` are unnecessary outside `push`'s schedule-
  detached flow but still work.

### Gap list (refreshed for 0.62.0+)

The original pre-design gaps are now resolved by the
negotiated decisions (Decisions blocks under each axis).
This list captures the **implementation-cycle gaps** the
design surfaces — concrete work for 0.62.0+ Todo entries.
Ordered (roughly) from cheapest equalization (smallest
blast radius) to largest, consistent with the
`## Commonality` ranking.

1. **`validate-desc` / `fix-desc` equalization** — local
   refactor: replace the `other_repo_from_config` prelude
   with a scope-aware resolution that no-ops when
   `Side::Bot` is absent. Body unchanged. Cheapest concrete
   equalization and the right prototype for the
   topology-from-config rule.
2. **`CommonArgs`-family por test coverage** — pure test
   work. Add `FixturePor` exercises to `chid` / `desc` /
   `show` / `list` tests. Surfaces bugs without
   architectural risk.
3. **`--dual` peer flag + internal enum** — add `--dual`
   (alias of "no `--por`"); make the value an enum, not
   a boolean. `--por` / `--dual` exactly-one-of. Threads
   through `init` / `clone` + becomes runtime override
   on every other subcommand.
4. **`--public` peer flag** — add `--public` as peer to
   `--private`. Exactly-one-of. Needed so users with
   `[default].private = true` in config can spell
   "public" per invocation.
5. **`--repo none` category** — third peer to `remote` /
   `local`. `init` / `clone` skip remote provisioning;
   `--account` skipped when `--repo none` resolves.
6. **Workspace `.vc-config.toml` write dropped under por**
   — today's degenerate `[workspace] path = "/"` write
   removed. Por never reads, never creates the workspace
   metadata file.
7. **`default_scope` broken-dual detection** — today's
   silent fallback to `Scope([Code])` when `.vc-config.toml`
   is missing in a `.claude/`-present workspace is a
   footgun. Add explicit broken-dual error. Prerequisite
   for the copying design's deferred-validation flow.
8. **Runtime `--por` on all subcommands** — add the flag
   to every subcommand (not just `init`/`clone`); thread
   it into `default_scope` as an override.
9. **`--init-from*` family + copying mechanism** — the
   broader file-copy design (see [[3]]). Subsumes
   `--use-template` (with back-compat shim) and the
   speculative `--config <path>` / `--gitignore <path>`.
   List-of-strings user-config support. Last-wins +
   warning. Canned-write suppression. Symlink/exec-bit
   preservation. `cp -a` semantics.
10. **`VC_X1_<KEY>` env-var resolution** — per-axis env
    vars in the resolution chain (between CLI and
    Local). See the Env-var table.
11. **`--no-local-config` / `--no-global-config` flags**
    — skip layer 3 / layer 4 escape hatches. Today's
    runtime `--por` covers the `--no-local-config` case
    for topology (only field local carries today).
12. **Baked-in default config + `vc-x1 config dump`** —
    `default-config.toml` compiled in via `include_str!`.
    `vc-x1 config dump` emits it for the user to save
    and modify. Removes the "Error" floor for natural-
    default axes.
13. **Local config redirection (`[global] config-path`,
    `[local] config-path`)** — load-with-visited-set,
    error on cycle.
14. **`finalize` standalone scope** — already supported
    by the body; gap is testing the standalone use
    (T runtime + `--scope` per-side) and documenting
    that `--detach` / `--delay` are no-ops outside
    `push`'s scheduled flow.

These become candidate `## Todo` entries that drive
0.62.0+ equalization cycles. The close-out commit seeds a
small subset; the rest live here for future Preparation
passes to pick up.

# References

[1]: /notes/todo.md
[2]: /notes/design-cli/por-dual-parity.md
[3]: /notes/design-cli/copying.md

content.text:
Took a careful pass through the audit doc, copying stub, the chores narrative, and the Gap list. Overall this is solid work — the decomposition is real and the next-step restraint is exactly right. A few places I'd push back on before code lands.

## What's genuinely good

- **Three-pass methodology** (audit → commonality inversion → axes). The inversion in `-2` is the cycle's pivotal move: reframed "we have a problem everywhere" to "the right pattern already exists, extend it." That mental flip is what made the rest tractable.
- **Axis decomposition is correct decomposition.** Calling out that `--por` is a *bundle* of (Topology, Vc-config, Remote, Privacy, Copying, Scaffolding) opens design space the bundle hid. The litmus is `audit.md:752` — "A user wanting a fully-plain single repo today can't spell it." That's a real defect of the bundle framing.
- **A2 collapse was earned.** You didn't keep `.vc-config.toml` write as an axis just because you initially listed it. The correlation argument is correct: presence of `.vc-config.toml` ≡ Topology. Honest housekeeping.
- **Two-class principle is sound** ("defaults where natural; errors only for user-specific keys"). This is the correct generalization of what `resolve_repo` already does. Baked-in default config + `vc-x1 config dump` closes the "what does layer-4-empty mean?" question elegantly.
- **T7-only promotion.** Resisting the urge to seed all 14 Gaps into Todo is the right call. Cheapest concrete equalization that validates the topology-from-config rule = good prototype choice.
- **Status note + "implementation will diverge" framing** (`audit.md:19-37`) is humility I'll thank you for in 0.65.0.

## Concerns I'd raise, ordered by conviction

### 1. Runtime `--por` everywhere is overloaded — I'd not ship this

`audit.md:530-534` says `--por` becomes a runtime override on every subcommand, short-circuiting `default_scope → Scope([Code])` regardless of workspace state. That mounts two meanings on one spelling:

- **Creation-time `--por`**: "this workspace is single."
- **Runtime `--por`**: "ignore the bot side for this invocation."

These are different things. Topology of an existing workspace is *not* mutable per-invocation (the `.claude/` dir either exists or doesn't). And the runtime escape already exists as `--scope code` / `-R .` for the `CommonArgs` family — the right pattern (per your own `## Commonality` summary).

**Push:** reserve `--por` for creation-time. For runtime "just my code," lean on `--scope code` (existing, well-understood) or add an unambiguous `--ignore-bot` if `--scope` doesn't fit the call site. This also makes Gap #8 ("Runtime `--por` on all subcommands") evaporate — and that's a *good* outcome; one fewer surface to maintain.

### 2. "Exactly-one-of `--por` / `--dual` with default-dual" is contradictory phrasing

`audit.md:521-522` says clap-enforced exactly-one-of; `:525` says default-dual. If neither flag is passed, either clap rejects (true exactly-one-of, friction for the common case) or it accepts (default kicks in, "exactly-one-of" is misleading). Same shape on `--private` / `--public` at `:643-647`.

**Push:** spell it "**at most one of**; absence → workspace-config or baked-in default." Honest about what the default does. Same fix for Privacy.

### 3. `--init-from-recursive` doubles the surface for the same capability

`copying.md:29-38` defines six flags: `--init-from`, `--init-from-code`, `--init-from-bot`, and `-recursive` variants of each. But recursion is a property of the *operand*, not the flag: `cp src/* dst/` is recursive whether you said `-r` or not; `cp src/foo.txt dst/` isn't. The shell expands the glob; vc-x1 walks each resolved path.

**Push:** one flag per side. `--init-from-code=<glob>` does the right thing per resolved path (file → file copy; dir → recursive copy). Cuts the surface in half, matches `cp` semantics users already have in their head. The "recursive" naming muddies what the flag controls vs. what the operand controls.

### 4. `--repo none` × Topology interaction isn't spelled out

The Remote axis Decisions (`audit.md:596-624`) introduces `--repo none` as a peer category, with `--account` skipped under it. Fine in isolation. But `--repo none` × `--dual` means "dual workspace with no remote for either side." The bot/sync/push workflow assumes remote-backed `.claude/`. Is dual+no-remote first-class, or rejected at parse time?

**Push:** add one paragraph to the Remote axis (or matrix footnotes) on the Topology × Remote=none interaction. Don't leave it implicit — a future user will try `init --dual --repo none` and get a confused error later in the workflow.

### 5. Resolution chain "CLI replaces config" for list-typed copying — call out the choice

`audit.md:697-699` says: "If user passes any `--init-from*` on CLI, the config-pinned list is ignored — match the broader chain rule."

The "broader chain rule" is about scalar values where "wins" unambiguously means "replaces." For list-valued axes, users coming from `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` / `PATH` expect merge, not replace. The replace choice is defensible (predictability beats expressivity) but it's not the obvious choice.

content.text:
Your framing makes sense and it's a better design than what the doc currently codifies. Let me trace where I went wrong.

The audit doc Topology Decisions block at `audit.md:530-534` reads:

> `--por` at runtime short-circuits `default_scope` → **`Scope([Code])`** regardless of what the workspace says. Escape hatch for "this command, just my code, leave `.claude/` alone."

That text describes asymmetric, code-privileged semantics: `→ Scope([Code])`, "just my code." I took that at face value and built the "two meanings on one spelling" critique on top of it.

Your framing is broader and symmetric:

- **Target-relative**, not side-relative. `--por` means "treat the repo I'm pointed at (`.` by default, or `-R <path>`) as a single repo — no sibling lookup."
- `--por -R .` (in a dual workspace) → operate on code, no sibling.
- `--por -R .claude` → operate on `.claude`, no sibling.
- `--por -R /some/vendored/single-repo` → operate on that repo as-is.

That gives `--por` **one** meaning everywhere: "this repo, on its own, no cross-repo expectations." Creation-time `--por` is a special case of the same rule (the target doesn't exist yet, so "treat as standalone" = "create as standalone").

Two things follow:

1. **My item 1 critique evaporates** under your framing. There's no overloading — there's one rule applied at two life stages. Retracting the concern.

2. **My "use `--scope code` instead" alternative was wrong** — it conflates two distinct operations:
   - `--scope code` selects *one side* of a known dual workspace.
   - `--por` declares the target itself isn't dual (no sibling exists, or pretend it doesn't).

   Different mental models. Not interchangeable.

What still needs doing: the **doc text doesn't match your intent**. `audit.md:530-534` currently codifies the narrow code-only reading. Fix at 0.62.0+ Preparation (or now if you want me to capture it before max review): rewrite that bullet as:

> Runtime override on every subcommand: `--por` declares the target repo (default `.`, or `-R/--repo <path>`) as single — no sibling traversal, no `.vc-config.toml > other-repo` lookup, no cross-repo ochid validation. Symmetric across code and bot — `--por` doesn't privilege a side, it suppresses the sibling-discovery step.

The "Por's view of the chain" cheat-sheet (`audit.md:897-913`) also reads code-asymmetric ("In a dual workspace, runtime `--por` short-circuits `default_scope` → `Scope([Code])`") and would need the same edit.

Want me to make that edit now (small, contained — touches audit doc only), or leave it for the 0.62.0 cycle that picks up T7?

  initially listed as independent; the design pass

  retired the axis. Honest housekeeping.

rejects (true exactly-one-of, friction for the common
case) or it accepts (default kicks in, "exactly-one-of"

that cycle starts.

bot/sync/push workflow assumes remote-backed `.claude/`.

first-class or rejected at parse time.

axis (or matrix footnotes) on the Topology × Remote=none
interaction. Don't leave it implicit. The bot thinks a
future user trying `init --dual --repo none` will hit a
confused error later in the workflow.

none` lands (Gap #5).

detection) is a *prerequisite* for Gap #9 (copying

validation to the first downstream subcommand, which
means broken-dual detection has to exist first.

**Proposed action:** one-line note above the list:
"ranking is rough; some structural prerequisites (e.g.
#7 before #9) override size ordering." Doesn't require
renumbering.

**Status:** open. Docs-only edit.

## Nits

- **`finalize` matrix row** marks T+SC support based on

  path exercises it today. The bot thinks adding a
  footnote — "currently latent — body supports it, no

toml` topology default that **opened** the cycle is, by

| --- | --- | --- | --- |
